summary: Matched the remaining assembly-backed code and data for the Target Test Mars and Ness ground/stage files, grtmars and grtness. The PR removed the separate asm data files, expressed StageCallbacks and StageData in C, converted remaining inline asm routines into C using known stage patterns, fixed a func_801C5DB0 prototype to take HSD_GObj*, and updated build lists so grtmars.c.o and grtness.c.o moved from asm-backed objects into epilogue-unscheduled source objects.

key_files.role: Converted remaining GrTMars code/data from inline asm and external asm data into C, including StageCallbacks, StageData, stage init, gobj setup, and callback routines.
Converted remaining GrTNess code/data from inline asm and external asm data into C using the same Target Test stage pattern as grtmars.
Deleted; former .data definitions for lbl_803E8EB0, /GrTMs.dat, lbl_803E8F0C, OSReport string, and source filename moved into C.
Deleted; former .data definitions for lbl_803E9030, /GrTNs.dat, lbl_803E908C, OSReport string, and source filename moved into C.
Updated function prototypes to static declarations with more precise parameter and return types.
Updated function prototypes to static declarations with more precise parameter and return types.
Corrected func_801C5DB0 prototype from struct _HSD_GObj by value to struct _HSD_GObj*.
Removed grtmars.s.o and grtness.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES because the assembly files were deleted.
Added grtmars.c.o and grtness.c.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED.

what_changed: Replaced assembly data tables with C declarations: static StageCallbacks lbl_803E8EB0/lbl_803E9030 arrays and StageData lbl_803E8F0C/lbl_803E908C.
Moved stage asset path strings into StageData initializers: "/GrTMs.dat" for GrTMars and "/GrTNs.dat" for GrTNess.

Converted stage startup routines to C, setting stage_info.unk8C.b4 = 0 and stage_info.unk8C.b5 = 1, spawning ids 0, 1, and 2, then calling func_801C39C0, func_801C3BB4, func_801C4210, and func_801C42AC.

Converted shared gobj setup logic to C in GrTMars_80221F98 and GrTNess_80222674: fetch gobj with func_801C14D0, clear Map callbacks at x8/xC, call GObj_SetupGXLink with func_801C5DB0, install callback3 to gp->x1C_callback, invoke callback0, and register callback2 through func_8038FD54.
Preserved failure OSReport behavior for missing gobjs with format "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", source file names grtmars.c/grtness.c, and line value 0xC3.
Converted small callback routines into C wrappers around func_801C8138, func_801C2ED0, func_801C2FE0, func_800115F4, and func_801CAE04.
Marked previously exported local stage helper routines as static in the headers and source.
Changed several return types from int to s32 or BOOL where the matched C made boolean intent explicit, such as GrTMars_802221C8 and GrTNess_802228A4 returning FALSE.
Added unused local arrays, such as u32 unused[2], in C versions of routines that call func_801C2ED0 and func_801C8138 to preserve matching stack shape.
Updated makefiles to remove obsolete asm objects and include the now-matching C objects in epilogue-unscheduled builds.
smart_moves: Used the similarity to previously matched GrT stages, as stated in the PR body, to translate both grtmars and grtness with nearly identical StageCallbacks, StageData, and gobj setup patterns.
Represented raw .4byte callback tables as StageCallbacks arrays instead of opaque int pointers, which made callback ordering and the 0xC0000000 field visible in C.
Kept labels for externally meaningful data objects, such as lbl_803E8F0C and lbl_803E908C, while making helper callbacks static to reduce unnecessary symbol exposure.
Used StageInfo, Map, HSD_GObj, StageCallbacks, and StageData types to replace raw offset manipulation like stage_info + 0x8C, gobj->0x2C, hsd_obj at 0x28, and map_id at offset 0x14.
Fixed func_801C5DB0 to accept an HSD_GObj pointer, matching its use as a GObj_SetupGXLink callback.
Preserved assembly-matching details that may look redundant in C, including unused stack locals and exact OSReport strings/source names.
Removed the asm object files from obj_files.mk at the same time as deleting the asm sources, preventing duplicate or stale data linkage.
decomp_lessons: For similar Target Test stage files, first compare against already matched GrT stages; callback tables and stage init logic may be reusable with only symbol names and dat filenames changed.
Raw stage data in assembly often maps cleanly to StageCallbacks arrays plus a StageData initializer. This can eliminate standalone .s data files once the struct layout is known.
Bit operations on stage_info.unk8C can become named bitfield assignments, here stage_info.unk8C.b4 = 0 and b5 = 1, if StageInfo is typed correctly.

GObj setup code for these stages follows a consistent pattern: func_801C14D0(id), clear Map x8/xC callbacks, GObj_SetupGXLink(gobj, func_801C5DB0, 3, 0), assign callback3 to x1C, run callback0, and optionally register callback2 via func_8038FD54 with priority 4.
When matching old asm that reports errors, keep exact string spelling and line constants; this PR preserves the nonstandard "couldn t" text and line 0xC3.
Function prototypes matter for matching and type correctness: passing struct _HSD_GObj by value was wrong for func_801C5DB0; the corrected pointer prototype supports GObj_SetupGXLink usage.
Headers for file-local stage helpers may still contain static prototypes if the C file needs declarations before data initializers reference the functions.
Do not remove apparently unused locals too aggressively; u32 unused[2] can be necessary to reproduce stack layout for matched code.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific symbols retain address-based decomp names with a capitalized stage prefix: GrTMars_80221EF8 and GrTNess_802225D4.
Data labels remained address labels where no semantic name was introduced: lbl_803E8EB0, lbl_803E8F0C, lbl_803E9030, and lbl_803E908C.
Local variables used short stage-decomp names such as gobj, gp, map, callbacks, cb, and id to reflect HSD_GObj, Map, StageCallbacks, and index roles.
Boolean-returning callbacks were typed as BOOL and returned FALSE/TRUE in new C where applicable.
Stage asset filenames use Target Test abbreviations: "/GrTMs.dat" for Mars and "/GrTNs.dat" for Ness.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted asm/melee/gr/grtmars.s and asm/melee/gr/grtness.s after moving their .data contents into C StageCallbacks and StageData initializers.
Replaced inline asm routines with C equivalents while preserving call order and constants from the disassembly.
Used static function declarations before data initializers so callback tables could reference functions defined later in the C file.
Matched callback table layout by initializing only three meaningful StageCallbacks entries in a four-element array, leaving trailing zero initialization to stand in for NULL words from assembly.
Kept 0xC0000000 in the third callback entry to match the raw table data.
Used typed field access, such as gobj->user_data, gobj->hsd_obj, gp->map_id, gp->x8_callback, gp->xC_callback, and gp->x1C_callback, to replace hardcoded offsets.
Inserted unused local arrays in routines equivalent to former stack-using asm functions to maintain stack allocation behavior.
Moved grtmars.c.o and grtness.c.o into EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED, suggesting the C now matched except for epilogue scheduling constraints.
follow_up_queries: Find other GrT stage PRs that use the same StageCallbacks and StageData pattern.
Search for remaining asm/melee/gr/grt*.s files with only .data callback tables.
Audit func_801C5DB0 declarations and call sites for HSD_GObj pointer type consistency.
Search for OSReport("%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n") in other stage files to reuse the gobj setup decomp pattern.
Find stage files still using extern int* stage_info instead of extern StageInfo stage_info.
